component.name: MetricStrip
component.description: A single bordered surface displaying related metrics as columns separated by vertical dividers. Communicates that the values are facets of one concept (not independent KPIs), occupies less visual real estate than a card grid, and condenses cleanly for persistent/sticky contexts.

useCases: Metrics that roll up into one concept — a shared border with only dividers between values signals that they belong together more clearly than separate cards do
Visually de-emphasized metric displays that shouldn't compete for eye-tracking real estate the way a grid of elevated cards does
Persistent or sticky contexts where metrics need to stay accessible regardless of scroll position and the strip can condense compactly
Billing summaries, order totals, account status — any scenario where the values are facets of a single entity, not independent KPIs

commonPatterns.name: with-header
commonPatterns.description: Metric strip with a title row and description
commonPatterns.composition: <MetricStrip><MetricStripHeader title="This Period" description="Jan 01 – Jan 31, 2026" /><MetricStripContent><MetricStripItem value="$4,847" label="Estimated charges" /><MetricStripItem value="$484,723" label="Revenue processed" /><MetricStripItem value="19 days" label="Remaining" /></MetricStripContent></MetricStrip>

commonPatterns.name: without-header
commonPatterns.description: Metric strip with just the data row, no title
commonPatterns.composition: <MetricStrip><MetricStripContent><MetricStripItem value="$120" label="Subtotal" /><MetricStripItem value="$9.60" label="Tax" /><MetricStripItem value="$129.60" label="Total" /></MetricStripContent></MetricStrip>

antiPatterns.scenario: Using MetricStrip for independent metrics that don't relate to each other
antiPatterns.reason: The shared surface and divider-only separation signals that these values are parts of one concept. Independent KPIs lose that meaning when forced into a single container.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use Card + StatBlock in a grid — separate elevated cards signal independent metrics

antiPatterns.scenario: Using MetricStrip when metrics need trend indicators, actions, or expandable detail
antiPatterns.reason: MetricStrip is intentionally a read-only summary. Adding interactivity or trend chrome contradicts its low-emphasis purpose.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use Card + StatBlock (for trends/actions) or MetricPanel (for expandable detail)

antiPatterns.scenario: Using a card grid for related metrics that should be visually quiet
antiPatterns.reason: A grid of individually elevated cards takes up more eye-tracking real estate and implies independence. When the metrics are facets of one concept and should sit in the background, the card grid over-emphasizes them.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use MetricStrip — single surface, no elevation, compact footprint

slots.MetricStripHeader: Optional title row with heading and description, followed by a horizontal divider
slots.MetricStripContent: Horizontal row of MetricStripItem children separated by vertical dividers
slots.MetricStripItem: A single metric column with a data value and a label

responsive.mobile: Consider stacking or reducing to fewer columns on narrow viewports
responsive.desktop: Horizontal row of equal-width metric columns

accessibility.role: Generic container — no special ARIA roles needed
accessibility.screenReader: Values and labels are read in source order

rules: Use MetricStrip when the values are facets of one concept — the shared surface and divider-only separation communicates that relationship
Use Card + StatBlock in a grid when metrics are independent KPIs — separate elevated cards signal independence
Use MetricPanel when metrics need expandable detail content (charts, tables)
MetricStrip has no elevation by design — it stays visually quiet so it doesn't compete with primary content for eye-tracking real estate
MetricStrip condenses well for sticky/persistent headers — use it when metrics need to stay accessible regardless of scroll position
MetricStripHeader is optional — omit for simple data-only displays
All MetricStripItem children go inside a single MetricStripContent — the content component handles vertical dividers automatically

aiHints.context: Choose MetricStrip over a card grid when: (1) the metrics are facets of one concept and the shared surface communicates that better than separate cards, (2) the metrics should be visually quiet and not compete for eye-tracking real estate, or (3) the metrics need to persist on screen (sticky headers) and the strip condenses compactly. Unlike Card + StatBlock (elevated, independent) or MetricPanel (expandable), MetricStrip is flat and compact — a single bordered container with only dividers between values.
